info.description: Frost v1 is a RESTful Web API for storing and retrieving weather- and climate observations.
info.title: Frost v1

200.description: Overall information about the service was successfully downloaded.

description.example: Checked stock of greetings and we are good.

get.summary: Health status of the Frost service.

get.description: Retrieve time series headers and (optionally) observations from time series type 'frost0'.

parameters.description: The sources to get data for as a comma-separated list of names. Only time series with a source that matches at least one of the names will be considered. Matching is case-insensitive and a name may contain asterisks for wildcard matching.
parameters.name: sources

parameters.description: The sensor levels to get data for as a comma-separated list of names. Only time series with a sensor level that matches at least one of the names will be considered. Matching is case-insensitive and a name may contain asterisks for wildcard matching.
parameters.name: sensorlevels

parameters.description: A [geo search](/docs/parameters#nearest) parameter to look for observations around a geographic point. __Example__: {"maxdist":7.5,"maxcount":3,"points":[{"lon":10.72, "lat":59.94275}]}
parameters.name: nearest

parameters.description: A [geo search](/docs/parameters#polygon) parameter to look for observations inside a polygon. __Example__: [{"lat":59.93,"lon":10.05},{"lat":59.93,"lon":11}, {"lat":60.25,"lon":10.77}]
parameters.name: polygon

parameters.description: A [geo search](/docs/parameters#inside) parameter used for selecting observations inside a region. __Example__: [{"type":"circle","lon":10.05,"lat":59.93,"radius":7.5}]
parameters.name: inside

parameters.description: A [geo search](/docs/parameters#outside) parameter used for selecting observations outside a region. __Example__: [{"type":"circle","lon":10.05,"lat":59.93,"radius":7.5}]
parameters.name: outside

parameters.description: A [geo search](/docs/parameters#geopostype) parameter used to decide whether to consider (e.g. apply geo filtering to) geo positions in time series headers ("stationary") or observation bodies ("mobile"). __Default value__: stationary
parameters.name: geopostype

parameters.description: The elements to get data for as a comma-separated list of names. Only time series with an element that matches at least one of the names will be considered. Matching is case-insensitive and a name may contain asterisks for wildcard matching.
parameters.name: elements

parameters.description: A [time specification](/docs/parameters#time) to select relevant observation times. Either a time range formated as "2020-01-01T00:00:00Z/2020-01-02T23:59:59Z", or the keyword 'latest' can be used (for the latter case, see also latestmaxage and latestlimit).
parameters.name: time

parameters.description: Used for a [time specification](/docs/parameters#latestmaxage) in 'latest' mode, this is the maximum age (relative to the time of the request) that the most recent observation can have in order to contribute to the result. Specify either a number of seconds or an [ISO 8601 duration] (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601#Durations). __Default value__: PT3H
parameters.name: latestmaxage

parameters.description: Used for a [time specification](/docs/parameters#latestlimit) in 'latest' mode, this is the maximum number of observations that may be included (if incobs==true) in the result for each contributing time series. __Default value__: 1
parameters.name: latestlimit

parameters.description: The maximum number of time series headers and individual observations that the response can contain. The effective limit to use will be min(itemlimit, <limit defined by server>) if itemlimit is specified, and <limit defined by server> if not.
parameters.name: itemlimit

parameters.description: Specify false if you only want time series headers (= metadata that don't vary with observation time) in the response. Specify true to additionally get observation values and metadata that vary with observation time.
parameters.name: incobs

sensorlevel.description: Sensor level in meters above ground.

time.description: Observation time. An ISO 8601 UTC time of the form 'YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ'. Seconds is currently the finest resolution supported.

200.description: Time series headers and (optionally) observations were successfully downloaded.

schema.title: Error details for 500 Internal Server Error.

post.description:
This method allows a dataset of time series type 'frost0' to be applied to the Frost service. Depending on its time T and body B, each observation in the dataset has the following effect on the storage:
ConditionEffect
No observation at T exists. The observation is inserted.
An observation at T exists already. The observation body is updated with B.
B is empty (i.e. [] or null). The observation at T (if any) is deleted.

sensorlevel.description: Sensor level in meters above ground.

time.description: Observation time. An ISO 8601 UTC time of the form 'YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ'. Seconds is currently the finest resolution supported.

200.description: Observations were successfully inserted/updated/deleted in existing time series.

schema.title: Error details for 500 Internal Server Error.

post.summary: Insert, update, or delete observations in existing time series

get.description:
This method gets status relevant for time series type 'frost0'.
NOTE: this is intended for internal use only.

schema.title: Internal status relevant for time series type 'frost0' successfully downloaded.

post.description: This method creates a set of time series of type 'frost0'. Nothing happens for any time series that already exists. The 'observations' array in the dataset is irrelevant and can be empty (i.e. [] or null).

sensorlevel.description: Sensor level in meters above ground.

accepted.description: number of time series accepted, i.e. not rejected

applied.description: number of time series applied, i.e. created (or ensured already existing)

rejected.description: number of time series rejected, e.g. due to write restrictions

schema.title: Time series successfully rejected/accepted/applied for the purpose of creation.

200.description: One or more new time series were either successfully created or already existing.

schema.title: Error details for 500 Internal Server Error.

post.description: This method deletes a set of time series of type 'frost0' (along with all observations, so be careful!). Nothing happens for any time series that doesn't exist. The 'observations' array in the dataset is irrelevant and can be empty (i.e. [] or null).

sensorlevel.description: Sensor level in meters above ground.

accepted.description: number of time series accepted, i.e. not rejected

applied.description: number of time series applied, i.e. deleted

rejected.description: number of time series rejected, e.g. due to write restrictions

schema.title: Time series successfully rejected/accepted/applied for the purpose of deletion.

200.description: One or more time series were either successfully deleted or already non-existing.

schema.title: Error details for 500 Internal Server Error.

post.description: This method updates the 'extra' part of a set of time series of type 'frost0'. Nothing happens for any time series that doesn't exist. The 'observations' array in the dataset is irrelevant and can be empty (i.e. [] or null).

sensorlevel.description: Sensor level in meters above ground.

accepted.description: number of time series accepted, i.e. not rejected

applied.description: number of time series applied, i.e. updated

rejected.description: number of time series rejected, e.g. due to write restrictions

schema.title: Time series successfully rejected/accepted/applied for the purpose of update.

200.description: One or more time series were either successfully updated or did not exist.

schema.title: Error details for 500 Internal Server Error.
